summary.conclusion: The three no-timeout failures split into source/gold-boundary issues for items 1 and 37, and a cleaner VMC answer-accuracy failure for item 14.
notes: All three no-timeout probes emitted parseable JSON.
Item 37 has duplicated option label g in the source prompt.
Item 1 expected answer appears physically inconsistent with a standard spinful triangular-lattice CDW interpretation.
Item 14 is the cleanest model/domain failure among the three.

probes.name: no_timeout_item_1
probes.result: JSON emitted after 420.267 s with actual U_0/3+2U_1
probes.interpretation: timeout was not the final failure; answer/gold interpretation dominates

probes.name: no_timeout_item_14
probes.result: JSON emitted after 420.271 s with actual b
probes.interpretation: model selected a plausible baseline-bias explanation but missed benchmark key d;e

probes.name: no_timeout_item_37
probes.result: JSON emitted after 120.134 s with actual b;c;d
probes.interpretation: model selected standard tensor-network sequence; source option labels are inconsistent

items.observed_failure: Kimi emitted U_0/3+2U_1 while the source key expects U_1/2.
items.root_cause_hypothesis: The prompt suggests spinful triangular-lattice CDW physics, for which Kimi's U_0/3+2U_1 interpretation is plausible; the source key U_1/2 may correspond to a different hard-core/spinless or occupancy-counting formulation.

evidence.value: Kimi reasoned about three-sublattice CDW energy U0/3+2U1, uniform energy 3U1, and boundary U0=3U1.

items.recommended_action: Audit original item 0 source/gold rationale before counting this as model error.
items.retest_plan: After audit, rerun item 1 with clarified spinful/CDW/hard-core assumptions.

items.observed_failure: Kimi emitted b while the source key expects d;e.
items.root_cause_hypothesis: Kimi focused on the standard VMC missing-baseline covariance term and rejected support mismatch; the benchmark expects support mismatch and high variance.

evidence.value: Kimi derived dE/dalpha = 2[<O E_L> - <O><E_L>] and selected option b.

items.recommended_action: Use this as a focused model/domain failure and build VMC support-mismatch probes.
items.retest_plan: Run simplified VMC baseline/support probes, then rerun item 14.

items.observed_failure: Kimi emitted b;c;d while the source key expects g, and the source prompt repeats option label g.
items.root_cause_hypothesis: The written physics task asks for the standard tensor-network sequence, while the gold key selects an ambiguous duplicated g option.

evidence.value: Kimi explicitly noticed the duplicated g option and selected coarse-grain, transfer matrix, eigenvalue spectrum.

items.recommended_action: Quarantine or repair item 36 before using it in agent-iteration scoring.
items.retest_plan: Inspect original source generation and relabel options before rerun.

recommended_iterations.action: Audit CMT source rows 0 and 36 before treating them as model failures.
recommended_iterations.why: Both show strong signs of prompt/gold or option-label mismatch.
recommended_iterations.retest: Rerun audited items with unchanged verifier or exclude them if ambiguous.

recommended_iterations.action: Build simplified VMC/RNN probes around item 14.
recommended_iterations.why: Item 14 is the cleanest model reasoning miss in the no-timeout slice.
recommended_iterations.retest: Run baseline/support/suppport-plus-variance VMC probes and compare choices.

recommended_iterations.action: Add an ambiguous-item flag to the CMT50 index and score reports.
recommended_iterations.why: Data-quality failures should not be aggregated as ordinary model failures.
recommended_iterations.retest: Regenerate score summaries with clean vs quarantined subsets.
